architecture.summary: Synchromesh is a two-stage framework for improving code generation from frozen pre-trained LLMs (GPT-3, Codex). First, Target Similarity Tuning (TST) fine-tunes a Sentence-BERT model to retrieve few-shot examples by matching program structure (AST similarity) rather than surface natural language similarity. Second, Constrained Semantic Decoding (CSD) guides token-by-token generation by querying domain-specific Completion Engines (CEs) that encode syntactic and semantic constraints, using Brzozowski derivatives to align LLM tokenization with language-valid token sequences. Neither component requires retraining the base LM.
trained_components: Target Similarity Tuning model (fine-tuned Sentence-BERT for program similarity)

biblio.title: Synchromesh: Reliable code generation from pre-trained language models

claims.statement: CSD improves execution accuracy on SQL from 56% to 61% for Codex 175B
CSD improves validity (executable programs) in SQL from 73% to 85% for Codex 175B
CSD eliminates execution errors in 29% of SQL queries generated by GPT-3 13B (validity improves from 43% to 72%)
CSD + TST improves exact-match accuracy in SMCalFlow from 45% to 63% for Codex 175B
In Vega-Lite, GPT-3 13B produces valid charts for 55% of queries unconstrained; CSD eliminates all errors (100% valid)
CSD reduces average normalized tree edit distance from 0.41 to 0.18 for GPT-3 175B in SMCalFlow
CSD adds an average of 8% overhead to the sampling procedure
TST cannot guarantee elimination of conceptual errors; when conceptual errors occur, CSD is usually insufficient to correct the prediction
